title: eriogonum trachygonum subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum trachygonum subsp. typicum, also known as Mormon tea, is a type of shrub or small tree native to the southwestern regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. Drinking the tea made from the leaves of this plant can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and sore throats, as well as provide relief from digestive issues and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Eriogonum trachygonum subsp. typicum can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by drying the leaves to create a herbal supplement.
